summary: Recording the os.Stdout/os.Stderr swap in runSyncResourceQuiet as a known limitation deferred to a syncResource(io.Writer, ...) refactor across all 3 call sites.
reason: Greptile P1 round-3 on PR #595 raised that swapping process-global os.Stdout/Stderr affects every goroutine in the process, not just the runSyncResourceQuiet caller. The existing mitigation (sync.Mutex serializing the swap; PATCH comment naming the constraint) bounds the blast radius to the autorefresh path which is single-goroutine today. The principled fix is to thread an io.Writer through syncResource(c, db, resource, sinceTS, full, maxPages, latestOnly, userParams) so output goes to a per-call sink. That touches 3 caller sites (channel_workflow, sync, sync_autorefresh) and ~15 fmt.Fprintf(os.Stdout, ...) emissions inside syncResource and its helpers; out of scope for this PR.
